title: Tyson Foods: Further Pressure Ahead Although Large Downside Priced In
text: Tyson Foods' sales bounced back from early last year's slump, and continue investing in its operation upgrades. Its financials are deteriorating too quickly in recent quarters due to increased capex and costs.

title: What Are Consumer Staples Stocks?
text: But what are consumer staples stocks, and why should you consider adding them to your investment portfolio? We will teach you how and why consumer staples stocks are essential.

title: Tyson Foods: Attractive Valuation Despite Inflation Headwinds
text: Inflation has been a headwind, and Tyson Foods' adjusted EPS declined 70% year over year in the first quarter of fiscal 2023. A recession could decrease earnings given the company's pricing power isn't that strong.

title: Tyson Foods Signs Agreement to Acquire Williams Sausage Company
text: SPRINGDALE, Ark., Feb. 21, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) has signed an agreement to acquire Williams Sausage Company, Inc. of Union City, Tenn., both companies announced today.

title: Tyson Foods Stock Is Down 40% From Its High. Time to Buy?
text: The market's forgetting the highly cyclical nature of the meat business.

title: Bert's February 2023 Dividend Stock Watch List
text: The goal of our stock screener is to identify if a stock is an undervalued dividend growth stock to buy. 2023 has not been kind to Pfizer. Pfizer's stock price is down over 14% year to date.

title: Live Cattle Prices Calling the Shots at Tyson Foods
text: Earnings per share in Tyson Foods Inc. (NYSE: $TSN) have declined from $3.07 per diluted during the first quarter of 2022 to an astonishing $0.88 in the first quarter of 2023 (that's a 72% decline!). To understand if this will be the new norm for the food giant, we need to understand what is brewing - or mooing - trouble.

title: Tyson Foods Holds 60th Annual Meeting of Shareholders
text: SPRINGDALE, Ark., Feb. 09, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tyson Foods (NYSE: TSN) held its 60th annual meeting of shareholders earlier today at its Emma St. location, the site of the company's original headquarters, to address company business following its first quarter results.

title: Afternoon of Internet & Videogame Earnings: PINS, ATVI & More
text: We see some interesting earnings results after the closing bell today.

title: Markets Drop for Second-Straight Day on Fed Hike Fears
text: U.S. stock markets opened the trading week with declines on Monday, Feb. 6, on concerns about additional tightening moves by the Federal Reserve.

title: Tyson Foods, Inc. (TSN) Q1 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE:TSN ) Q1 2023 Earnings Conference Call February 6, 2023 9:00 AM ET Company Participants Sean Cornett - Vice President, Investor Relations Donnie King - President and Chief Executive Officer John Tyson - Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Brady Stewart - Group President, Fresh Meats Stewart Glendinning - Group President, Prepared Foods Wes Morris - Group President, Poultry Amy Tu - President, International and Chief Administrative Officer Conference Call Participants Alexia Howard - Bernstein Ken Goldman - JPMorgan Ben Bienvenu - Stephens Adam Samuelson - Goldman Sachs Robert Moskow - Credit Suisse Ben Theurer - Barclays Michael Lavery - Piper Sandler Eric Larson - Seaport Research Partners Operator Good morning and welcome to the Tyson Foods First Quarter 2023 Earnings Conference Call. [Operator Instructions] Please note today's event is being recorded.

title: Tyson Foods falls after earnings tumble as inflation-weary customers are buying less meat
text: "We got hit in the mouth in Q1 because of all the protein on the market," Tyson CEO Donnie King said in a Monday call with investors.

title: Tyson Foods takes profit hit as beef prices fall
text: Tyson Foods saw profits tumble in its fiscal first quarter after declining beef prices, waning demand for pork and higher costs took a toll on earnings.

title: Tyson Foods shares tank on weak demand for chicken
text: Tyson Foods took a “hit in the mouth” as larger-than-expected US beef and pork supplies reduced demand for its chicken, company executives said on Monday, as the meatpacker missed Wall Street estimates for quarterly profits. Shares were down 4.7% as Tyson also cut its outlooks for operating margins this year.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Q1 Earnings Lag Estimates, Sales Increase
text: Tyson Foods' (TSN) first-quarter fiscal 2023 results reflect increased sales due to higher average prices and volumes. However, earnings declined from the year-ago quarter's figure.

title: Tyson Foods' shares fall as lower beef prices chew into 1Q earnings
text: Tyson Foods (NYSE:TSN) stock fell in early trade after the protein producer reported first-quarter sales and earnings that were below market estimates. The Arkansas-based company reported sales of $13.3 billion for the three months to December 31, 2022, 2.5% higher than the same quarter a year earlier but below the $13.5 billion pencilled in by analysts.

title: What Tyson Foods Q1 Means For Staples Stocks
text: Tyson Foods (NASDAQ: TSN) Q1 report has put the fear of shrinking margins into the Consumer Staples (NYSEARCA: XLP) market but the takeaway is clear. The issues impacting Tyson Foods are unique to the company and centered in the beef, pork and chicken markets so fear of contagion is limited.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Q1 Earnings Miss Estimates
text: Tyson (TSN) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -37.04% and 1.12%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Tyson Stock Slides After Earnings Fall Well Short of Expectations
text: Tyson posts first-quarter adjusted earnings of 85 cents a share on sales of $13.26 billion. Analysts were expecting earnings of $1.31 a share.

title: Tyson Foods stock slides premarket after earnings miss by a wide margin
text: Tyson Foods Inc. stock TSN, -1.69% slid 5.9% in premarket trade Monday, after the meat supplier missed consensus estimates for its fiscal first quarter by a wide margin. “We faced some challenges in the first quarter,” Chief Executive Donnie King said in a statement.

title: Tyson Foods Reports First Quarter 2023 Results
text: Company delivers record high first quarter sales performance Company delivers record high first quarter sales performance

title: For the Seventh Consecutive Year, Tyson Foods Tops FORTUNE World's Most Admired Companies List for Food Production
text: SPRINGDALE, Ark., Feb. 01, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) ranks No. 1 on Fortune magazine's list of the World's Most Admired Companies in the Food Production category for the seventh consecutive year.
